the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog twice in daylight
multi token prediction can expose useful future signal in hidden states
layer routing should prefer different depth bands for different horizons
deterministic smoke tests prevent expensive debugging later in the project
sparse routers keep only a small active set of layers for each horizon
dense mixtures may average incompatible semantic representations together
probe heatmaps can reveal whether future information is already present
a local toy backbone is enough to validate config parsing and tensor shapes
evaluation must record future metrics acceptance metrics and router metrics
every result should be tied to a run manifest and resolved config file
careful selection rules avoid paper drift and accidental cherry picking
negative results are still useful when they are well validated and explained
the acceptance proxy advances by exactly one base greedy token each step
stateless layer norm is the canonical hidden state normalization here
warmup schedules may help harder future horizons stabilize during training
a confirmatory run can be smaller if the main budget is tight
plots and tables must always come from canonical registries not terminal logs
the final paper should contain only evidence backed by saved artifacts
the implementation should follow the package literally and log any necessary assumptions
